markers.title: Unfilled template placeholder visible to stakeholders
markers.body: 'dd/mm/yyyy' appears literally in the notification body — this is an unformatted date string, not a real date. Replace with a concrete demo date (e.g. '30 Jun 2026') before any stakeholder review.

markers.title: Three identical notifications with same timestamp
markers.body: Three 'Demand Plus invoice reconciliation due' notifications appear back-to-back with identical body text and the same timestamp (10:14 am, The Ecofern Hotel). This reads as a data loop bug, not realistic volume. Use varied content and staggered timestamps to tell a credible notification story.

markers.title: 'Payment' filter chip clipped — no scroll signal
markers.body: The last filter chip ('Payment') is cut off at the right edge with no gradient or indicator that the row scrolls. Horizontal scroll with a fade at the edge is the expected pattern; without it users assume the list ends where it clips.

markers.title: 'Earlier' has a single item from 60 days ago
markers.body: The 90-day window shows only one historical notification dated 27 April — two months old. A single item under 'Earlier' suggests the notification history is sparse or broken. Seed 2–3 realistic older notifications to demonstrate the pattern meaningfully.

markers.title: 'Select' and '...' actions have no clear scope
markers.body: 'Select' (coral) and '...' (ellipsis) sit in the header with no supporting label. It's unclear whether 'Select' starts multi-select mode, and what '...' contains (mark all read? Settings?). Label the ellipsis or expose its most common action inline.

working_well: Red dot vs grey dot for unread vs read is an immediately recognisable convention.
Today / Earlier grouping gives the list a clear temporal structure without dates on every row.
'All properties' dropdown at the top is a logical scope control for multi-property accounts.
Notification rows are tall enough to tap comfortably and body text wraps predictably.
worth_considering: Add a 'Mark all as read' shortcut — the most common notification action on a busy morning.
Show a count badge on each filter chip (e.g. 'Billing (2)') so staff can triage by category without switching tabs.
Consider a swipe-to-dismiss gesture on individual rows to mirror familiar iOS notification patterns.
